VP Engineering Delivery initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.
XR Systems Architect initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.
Use mongodb-memory-server as the local MongoDB repository contract test path, with MongoMemoryReplSet for transaction-sensitive behavior and production URI tests gated separately.
Android Platform Tools were installed locally through Homebrew; adb 37.0.0 can see the Quest 3 and reverse port 5173 for local Vite testing.
The verification ladder should include mongodb-memory-server repository tests and a Quest 3 manual smoke result, each with pass/skip/blocker status.
Use the user-attached Quest 3 over USB-C, adb authorization, Quest Browser, and local port forwarding as the first manual WebXR device smoke gate.
OpenClinXR may reference public Step 2 CS structure only as historical inspiration; it must not claim USMLE, ECFMG, NBME, AMA, licensure, or high-stakes equivalence or endorsement.
Synthetic voice and virtual patient dialogue can create disclosure, consent, misuse, provenance, and overreliance risks even in training simulations.
Model and voice providers require audit records before learner exposure, including provider ID, runtime/model, request ID, latency, and safety status.
Nurse interruption, escalation handling, and teamwork communication must remain first-class trace requirements in the first ED station.
The main code-phase failure mode is over-scaffolding many packages without proving a runnable station lifecycle.
The first implementation sprint must be blocked by a deterministic ED chest pain station simulation, not by package scaffolding volume.

Use small Qwen GGUF or MLX-compatible models for first Apple Silicon smoke tests, treat DeepSeek distills as follow-on reasoning candidates, and keep Kimi-class models as server/offload research candidates.
The local hardware spike ran on an M1 Max with 64 GB RAM, so M4Pro/M4Max performance assumptions remain evidence debt until target hardware benchmarks run.
The first demo must show faculty review and debrief value, not only immersive learner presentation.
Optional XR, local AI, voice, and asset-generation packages need license, install, and runtime-risk intake before entering default setup.
Optional runtimes should report machine-readable not_configured or skipped statuses and must not fail baseline verification.
Voice cloning or realistic local speech synthesis increases misuse, consent, and learner trust risks; mock voice should remain acceptable for first slice.
VibeVoice is a promising local voice research candidate but must remain behind disclosure, consent, provenance, and misuse gates before learner exposure.
The first implementation should proceed through small tested commits from workspace to schemas, domain, fixture, trace, review, harness, API, admin, XR, providers, assets, and verification.
StableGen GPL-3.0 and MakeHuman/MPFB AGPL/GPL source create runtime and distribution risk; asset ingestion must enforce provenance, output license review, and forbidden runtime dependency tags.
Stations must support realistic environments with additional actors, interruptions, emotional/pain responses, urgent deterioration, team communication, and post-encounter documentation.
OpenClinXR score outputs remain formative or local-programmatic until a score-use statement, rater training, difficulty metadata, trace-quality metric, and validation evidence exist.
Trace completeness is a prerequisite for defensible evidence but is not validity evidence by itself.
Use Penpot/OpenPencil for design exploration and React, Ant Design 6, Pro Components, Storybook, Serenity/JS, Playwright, and Vitest for dense non-examinee authoring, review, and trace workflows.

The first case bank should include 12 reviewed specialty stations with environment, actors, hidden truth, event schedule, 3D assets, trace tags, and explicit communication-style profiles for empathy, de-escalation, and team-pressure testing.

Runtime dependencies and asset pipeline outputs should prefer MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-style, and CC0/CC-BY assets; MPL tools may be standalone, while GPL/AGPL code stays out of runtime/distributed product.

Model, voice, and local LLM capabilities should be exposed through auditable provider adapters, while every actor card carries a communication profile, bounded-stubbornness policy, and emotion/style QA hooks.

Iteration 0003 source ledger adds original asset bundle findings, local virtual-patient PDFs, current technology/license checks, WebXR/WebTransport performance references, provider docs, and npm package metadata; unresolved evidence remains Quest 3 device benchmarks, provider privacy/latency, and final asset-output licensing.
Use ExamBlueprint, ExamForm, Station, ActorCard, StationRun, TraceEvent, ReviewPacket, and KnowledgeGraph nodes as the primary implementation boundaries for the clinical skills exam platform.

Iteration 0003 improves implementation readiness by adding technology, asset, provider, admin UX/testing, communication-style QA, and case-bank guidance while preserving open evidence debt for device, provider, license, and review-throughput validation.
Quest 3 runtime should load pre-baked GLB/KTX2/meshopt station bundles with no live body, texture, clothing, or full-motion generation; target 72 FPS and gate station approval on device smoke tests.

OpenClinXR's design baseline is now a source-backed sequential clinical skills exam architecture supporting 12 timed stations, note windows, breaks, human review, and a deterministic ED chest pain first proof slice.

Azure App Service Plan B1 should orchestrate one-user pilot sessions, REST, WebSocket control, trace batching, and provider calls, while heavy assets, inference, and animation generation run offline or outside B1.

MongoDB persistence should separate authored scenario truth from learner-visible station state, store append-only trace events and LLM audit events, and index knowledge nodes/edges for scenario generation and actor memory retrieval.

Iteration 0002 source ledger covers Step 2 CS public structure, ECFMG operational notices, AMA day-one skills, LLM virtual patient articles, patient-note corpus caution, and CellixJS DDD/monorepo docs; the supplied IEEE link remains unresolved evidence debt.
Use actor-like runtime cells for exam session, station, environment, patient, family, nurse, evaluator, and guardrail responsibilities, with immutable messages and auditable state transitions.
Public marketplace launch should remain outside the first MVP until scenario provenance and certification governance mature.
Quest XR should be treated as an M1 interaction slice after the M0 evidence workflow is clear.

actor-cell-runtime.summary: Use actor-like runtime cells for exam session, station, environment, patient, family, nurse, evaluator, and guardrail responsibilities, with immutable messages and auditable state transitions.

source-librarian.summary: Source Librarian initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.

Iteration 0003 source ledger adds original asset bundle findings, local virtual-patient PDFs, current technology/license checks, WebXR/WebTransport performance references, provider docs, and npm package metadata; unresolved evidence remains Quest 3 device benchmarks, provider privacy/latency, and final asset-output licensing.

Iteration 0002 source ledger covers Step 2 CS public structure, ECFMG operational notices, AMA day-one skills, LLM virtual patient articles, patient-note corpus caution, and CellixJS DDD/monorepo docs; the supplied IEEE link remains unresolved evidence debt.

specialty-advisory-board-chair.summary: Specialty Advisory Board Chair initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.

specialty-clinical-attacker.summary: Specialty Clinical Attacker initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.

supply-chain-dependency-attacker.summary: Supply Chain And Dependency Attacker initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.
Optional XR, local AI, voice, and asset-generation packages need license, install, and runtime-risk intake before entering default setup.

surgery-physician.summary: Surgery Physician initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.

test-automation-lead.summary: Test Automation Lead initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.
The verification ladder should include mongodb-memory-server repository tests and a Quest 3 manual smoke result, each with pass/skip/blocker status.
Optional runtimes should report machine-readable not_configured or skipped statuses and must not fail baseline verification.

ux-friction-critic.summary: UX Friction Critic initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.

ux-product-lead.summary: UX And Product Lead initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.
Use Penpot/OpenPencil for design exploration and React, Ant Design 6, Pro Components, Storybook, Serenity/JS, Playwright, and Vitest for dense non-examinee authoring, review, and trace workflows.

voice-safety-misuse-attacker.summary: Voice Safety And Misuse Attacker initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.
Voice cloning or realistic local speech synthesis increases misuse, consent, and learner trust risks; mock voice should remain acceptable for first slice.

voice-speech-engineer.summary: Voice And Speech Engineer initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.
VibeVoice is a promising local voice research candidate but must remain behind disclosure, consent, provenance, and misuse gates before learner exposure.

vp-engineering-delivery.summary: VP Engineering Delivery initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.
The first implementation should proceed through small tested commits from workspace to schemas, domain, fixture, trace, review, harness, API, admin, XR, providers, assets, and verification.

xr-systems-architect.summary: XR Systems Architect initialized with persistent memory and rubric-linked responsibilities.
Use the user-attached Quest 3 over USB-C, adb authorization, Quest Browser, and local port forwarding as the first manual WebXR device smoke gate.
Quest 3 runtime should load pre-baked GLB/KTX2/meshopt station bundles with no live body, texture, clothing, or full-motion generation; target 72 FPS and gate station approval on device smoke tests.
Use actor-like runtime cells for exam session, station, environment, patient, family, nurse, evaluator, and guardrail responsibilities, with immutable messages and auditable state transitions.
Quest XR should be treated as an M1 interaction slice after the M0 evidence workflow is clear.

workOrders.goal: Revise the implementation plan and code backlog around the weakest rubric dimensions and open debts.
workOrders.requiredOutput: Core revision memo with closed or explicitly re-owned score, evidence, and decision gaps.

workOrders.goal: Attack the revised plan and produce a stronger counterplan or required remediations.
workOrders.requiredOutput: Adversarial counterplan with blocking issues, outdo strategy, and testable acceptance gates.

workOrders.goal: Prepare blocker-focused leadership preflight without pretending the plan is ready for approval.
workOrders.requiredOutput: Leadership review packet or preflight blocker brief tied to the rubric.

nextActions.summary: Capture and validate a human worn-headset Quest manual performance report that proves immersive entry, readable in-scene text, controller/hand affordances, locomotion comfort, frame pacing, heat, and battery observations.
Run structured-output, hidden-truth, actor-policy, and target M4 Pro or M4 Max local model benchmarks before enabling local dialogue in station runtime.
Prove streaming local voice with first audible playback latency, WebXR playback, disclosure, retention, and misuse controls before live voice use.
Align IWSDK benchmark-gate IDs with scorecard debt and record Phase 2 devtools, MCP inventory, Vite peer-range, reference metadata, bundle, and Quest evidence before runtime adoption.
Add production-grade asset-pipeline reports for generated humans, skin, clothing, rigging, animation, equipment, optimization, and headset budgets before calling the asset lane production-ready.
Decide whether benchmark gate IDs should follow the original debt item or the current capability area when evidence evolves across iterations.
Stakeholders may treat local model and voice smoke results as live clinical simulation readiness.
Quest shell, CDP frame, and sidecar evidence may still be confused with worn-headset immersive comfort evidence.
The asset-job facade may be mistaken for production asset-generation readiness before rigging, animation, texture, LOD, collider, and headset budget evidence exists.
IWSDK sidecar momentum may pull incompatible or oversized packages into the primary XR runtime before MCP, bundle, peer-range, and Quest evidence clears.
Evidence ledger ID drift may cause leadership to believe a blocker was closed or reopened for the wrong reason.

guardrails: Prefer narrow rules with clear ownership and low false-positive risk.
